text: And it came to pass in the days<S>3117</S> when the judges<S>8199</S> judged<S>8199</S>, that there was a famine<S>7458</S> in the land<S>776</S>. And a certain man<S>376</S> of Beth-lehem<S>1035</S>-judah went<S>3212</S> to sojourn<S>1481</S> in the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>, he<S>802</S>, and his wife, and his two<S>8147</S> sons<S>1121</S>.

And the name<S>8034</S> of the man<S>376</S> was Elimelech<S>458</S>, and the name<S>8034</S> of his wife<S>802</S> Naomi<S>5281</S>, and the name<S>8034</S> of his two<S>8147</S> sons<S>1121</S> Mahlon<S>4248</S> and Chilion<S>3630</S>, Ephrathites<S>673</S> of Beth-lehem<S>1035</S>-judah. And they came<S>935</S> into the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>, and continued there.
And Elimelech<S>458</S>, Naomi<S>5281</S>'s husband<S>376</S>, died<S>4191</S>; and she was left<S>7604</S>, and her two<S>8147</S> sons<S>1121</S>.

And they took<S>5375</S> them wives<S>802</S> of the women<S>802</S> of Moab<S>4125</S>; the name<S>8034</S> of the one<S>259</S> was Orpah<S>6204</S>, and the name<S>8034</S> of the other<S>8145</S> Ruth<S>7327</S>: and they dwelt<S>3427</S> there about ten<S>6235</S> years<S>8141</S>.

And Mahlon<S>4248</S> and Chilion<S>3630</S> died<S>4191</S> both<S>8147</S> of them<S>802</S>; and the woman was left<S>7604</S> of her two<S>8147</S> children<S>3206</S> and of her husband<S>376</S>.

Then she arose<S>6965</S> with her daughters-in<S>3618</S>-law, that she might return<S>7725</S> from the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>: for she had heard<S>8085</S> in the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S> how that Jehovah<S>3068</S> had visited<S>6485</S> his people<S>5971</S> in giving<S>5414</S> them bread<S>3899</S>.

And she went forth<S>3318</S> out of the place<S>4725</S> where she was<S>8147</S>, and her two daughters-in<S>3618</S>-law with her<S>3212</S>; and they went on the way<S>1870</S> to return<S>7725</S> unto the land<S>776</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto her two<S>8147</S> daughters-in<S>3618</S>-law, Go<S>3212</S>, return<S>7725</S> each<S>802</S> of you to her mother<S>517</S>'s house<S>1004</S>: Jehovah<S>3068</S> deal<S>6213</S> kindly<S>2617</S> with you<S>6213</S>, as ye have dealt with the dead<S>4191</S>, and with me.

Jehovah<S>3068</S> grant<S>5414</S> you that ye may find<S>4672</S> rest<S>4496</S>, each<S>802</S> of you in the house<S>1004</S> of her husband<S>376</S>. Then she kissed<S>5401</S> them<S>5375</S>, and they lifted up their voice<S>6963</S>, and wept<S>1058</S>.
And they said<S>559</S> unto her<S>7725</S>, Nay, but we will return with thee unto thy people<S>5971</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S>, Turn again<S>7725</S>, my daughters<S>1323</S>: why will ye go<S>3212</S> with me<S>1121</S>? have I yet sons in my womb<S>4578</S>, that they may be your husbands<S>582</S>?

Turn again<S>7725</S>, my daughters<S>1323</S>, go<S>3212</S> your way<S>2204</S>; for I am too old to have a husband<S>376</S>. If I should say<S>559</S>, I have<S>3426</S> hope<S>8615</S>, if I should even have a husband<S>376</S> to-night<S>3915</S>, and should also bear<S>3205</S> sons<S>1121</S>;

would ye therefore tarry<S>7663</S> till they<S>3860</S> were grown<S>1431</S>? would ye therefore stay<S>5702</S> from having<S>3860</S> husbands<S>376</S>? nay<S>1323</S>, my daughters, for it grieveth<S>4843</S> me much<S>3966</S> for your sakes<S>3027</S>, for the hand of Jehovah<S>3068</S> is gone forth against me<S>3318</S>.
And they lifted up<S>5375</S> their voice<S>6963</S>, and wept again<S>1058</S>: and Orpah<S>6204</S> kissed<S>5401</S> her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law, but Ruth<S>7327</S> clave unto her<S>1692</S>.

And she said<S>559</S>, Behold<S>2994</S>, thy sister-in-law is gone back<S>7725</S> unto her people<S>5971</S>, and unto her god<S>430</S>: return<S>7725</S> thou after<S>310</S> thy sister-in<S>2994</S>-law.

And Ruth<S>7327</S> said<S>559</S>, Entreat<S>6293</S> me not to leave<S>5800</S> thee<S>7725</S>, and to return from following after<S>310</S> thee<S>3212</S>, for whither thou goest, I will go<S>3212</S>; and where thou lodgest<S>3885</S>, I will lodge<S>3885</S>; thy people<S>5971</S> shall be my people<S>5971</S>, and thy God<S>430</S> my God<S>430</S>;

where thou diest<S>4191</S>, will I die<S>4191</S>, and there will I be buried<S>6912</S>: Jehovah<S>3068</S> do<S>6213</S> so to me<S>3254</S>, and more also<S>4194</S>, if aught but death part thee and me<S>6504</S>.
And when she saw<S>7200</S> that she was stedfastly minded<S>553</S> to go<S>3212</S> with her<S>2308</S>, she left off speaking unto her<S>1696</S>.

So they two<S>8147</S> went<S>3212</S> until they came<S>935</S> to Beth-lehem<S>1035</S>. And it came to pass<S>935</S>, when they were come to Beth-lehem<S>1035</S>, that all the city<S>5892</S> was moved<S>1949</S> about them<S>559</S>, and the women said, Is this Naomi<S>5281</S>?
And she said<S>559</S> unto them<S>7121</S>, Call me not Naomi<S>5281</S>, call<S>7121</S> me Mara<S>4755</S>; for the Almighty<S>7706</S> hath dealt very<S>3966</S> bitterly with me<S>4843</S>.

I went out<S>1980</S> full<S>4392</S>, and Jehovah<S>3068</S> hath brought me home again<S>7725</S> empty<S>7387</S>; why call<S>7121</S> ye me Naomi<S>5281</S>, seeing Jehovah<S>3068</S> hath testified<S>6030</S> against me<S>7706</S>, and the Almighty hath afflicted me<S>7489</S>?

So Naomi<S>5281</S> returned<S>7725</S>, and Ruth<S>7327</S> the Moabitess<S>4125</S>, her daughter-in<S>3618</S>-law, with her<S>7725</S>, who returned out of the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>: and they came<S>935</S> to Beth-lehem<S>1035</S> in the beginning<S>8462</S> of barley<S>8184</S> harvest<S>7105</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> had a kinsman<S>4129</S> of her husband<S>376</S>'s, a mighty<S>1368</S> man<S>376</S> of wealth<S>2428</S>, of the family<S>4940</S> of Elimelech<S>458</S>, and his name<S>8034</S> was Boaz<S>1162</S>.

And Ruth<S>7327</S> the Moabitess<S>4125</S> said<S>559</S> unto Naomi<S>5281</S>, Let me now go<S>3212</S> to the field<S>7704</S>, and glean<S>3950</S> among the ears of grain<S>7641</S> after<S>310</S> him in whose sight<S>5869</S> I shall find<S>4672</S> favor<S>2580</S>. And she said<S>559</S> unto her<S>3212</S>, Go, my daughter<S>1323</S>.

And she went<S>3212</S>, and came<S>935</S> and gleaned<S>3950</S> in the field<S>7704</S> after<S>310</S> the reapers<S>7114</S>: and her hap<S>4745</S> was to light<S>7136</S> on the portion<S>2513</S> of the field<S>7704</S> belonging unto Boaz<S>1162</S>, who was of the family<S>4940</S> of Elimelech<S>458</S>.

And<S>1162</S>, behold, Boaz came<S>935</S> from Beth-lehem<S>1035</S>, and said<S>559</S> unto the reapers<S>7114</S>, Jehovah<S>3068</S> be with you<S>559</S>. And they answered him<S>3068</S>, Jehovah bless thee<S>1288</S>.
Then said<S>559</S> Boaz<S>1162</S> unto his servant<S>5288</S> that was set<S>5324</S> over the reapers<S>7114</S>, Whose damsel is this<S>5291</S>?

And the servant<S>5288</S> that was set<S>5324</S> over the reapers<S>7114</S> answered<S>6030</S> and said<S>559</S>, It is the Moabitish<S>4125</S> damsel<S>5291</S> that came back<S>7725</S> with Naomi<S>5281</S> out of the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>:

And she said<S>559</S>, Let me glean<S>3950</S>, I pray you<S>622</S>, and gather after<S>310</S> the reapers<S>7114</S> among the sheaves<S>6016</S>. So she came<S>935</S>, and hath continued<S>5975</S> even<S>227</S> from the morning<S>1242</S> until now<S>3427</S>, save that she tarried a little<S>4592</S> in the house<S>1004</S>.

Then said<S>559</S> Boaz<S>1162</S> unto Ruth<S>7327</S>, Hearest<S>8085</S> thou not<S>1323</S>, my daughter? Go<S>3212</S> not to glean<S>3950</S> in another<S>312</S> field<S>7704</S>, neither pass<S>5674</S> from hence<S>1692</S>, but abide here fast by my maidens<S>5291</S>.

Let thine eyes<S>5869</S> be on the field<S>7704</S> that they do reap<S>7114</S>, and go<S>1980</S> thou after<S>310</S> them<S>6680</S>: have I not charged the young men<S>5288</S> that they shall not touch<S>5060</S> thee<S>6770</S>? and when thou art athirst, go<S>1980</S> unto the vessels<S>3627</S>, and drink<S>8354</S> of that which the young men<S>5288</S> have drawn<S>7579</S>.

Then she fell<S>5307</S> on her face<S>6440</S>, and bowed<S>7812</S> herself to the ground<S>776</S>, and said<S>559</S> unto him<S>4672</S>, Why have I found favor<S>2580</S> in thy sight<S>5869</S>, that thou shouldest take knowledge<S>5234</S> of me<S>5237</S>, seeing I am a foreigner?

And Boaz<S>1162</S> answered<S>6030</S> and said<S>559</S> unto her<S>5046</S>, It hath fully been showed<S>5046</S> me<S>6213</S>, all that thou hast done unto thy mother-in<S>2545</S>-law since<S>310</S> the death<S>4194</S> of thy husband<S>376</S>; and how thou hast left<S>5800</S> thy father<S>1</S> and thy mother<S>517</S>, and the land<S>776</S> of thy nativity<S>4138</S>, and art come<S>3212</S> unto a people<S>5971</S> that thou knewest<S>3045</S> not heretofore<S>8543</S>.

Jehovah<S>3068</S> recompense<S>7999</S> thy work<S>6467</S>, and a full<S>8003</S> reward<S>4909</S> be given thee of Jehovah<S>3068</S>, the God<S>430</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, under whose wings<S>3671</S> thou art come<S>935</S> to take refuge<S>2620</S>.

Then she said<S>559</S>, Let me find<S>4672</S> favor<S>2580</S> in thy sight<S>5869</S>, my lord<S>113</S>, for that thou hast comforted<S>5162</S> me<S>1696</S>, and for that thou hast spoken kindly<S>3820</S> unto thy handmaid<S>8198</S>, though I be not as one<S>259</S> of thy handmaidens<S>8198</S>.

And at meal-time<S>6256</S> Boaz<S>1162</S> said<S>559</S> unto her<S>5066</S>, Come hither<S>1988</S>, and eat<S>398</S> of the bread<S>3899</S>, and dip<S>2881</S> thy morsel<S>6595</S> in the vinegar<S>2558</S>. And she sat<S>3427</S> beside<S>6654</S> the reapers<S>7114</S>, and they reached<S>6642</S> her parched<S>7039</S> grain<S>398</S>, and she did eat, and was sufficed<S>7646</S>, and left thereof<S>3498</S>.

And when she was risen up<S>6965</S> to glean<S>3950</S>, Boaz<S>1162</S> commanded<S>6680</S> his young men<S>5288</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Let her glean<S>3950</S> even among the sheaves<S>6016</S>, and reproach her not<S>3637</S>.
And also pull<S>7997</S> out some for<S>7997</S> her from the bundles<S>6653</S>, and leave<S>5800</S> it<S>3950</S>, and let her glean, and rebuke her not<S>1605</S>.
So she gleaned<S>3950</S> in the field<S>7704</S> until even<S>6153</S>; and she beat out<S>2251</S> that which she had gleaned<S>3950</S>, and it was about an ephah<S>374</S> of barley<S>8184</S>.

And she took it up<S>5375</S>, and went<S>935</S> into the city<S>5892</S>; and her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law saw<S>7200</S> what she had gleaned<S>3950</S>: and she brought forth<S>3318</S> and gave<S>5414</S> to her that which she had left<S>3498</S> after she was sufficed<S>7648</S>.

And her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law said<S>559</S> unto her<S>3950</S>, Where hast thou gleaned to-day<S>3117</S>? and where<S>375</S> hast thou wrought<S>6213</S>? blessed<S>1288</S> be he that did take knowledge<S>5234</S> of thee<S>5046</S>. And she showed her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law with whom she had wrought<S>6213</S>, and said<S>559</S>, The man<S>376</S>'s name<S>8034</S> with whom I wrought<S>6213</S> to-day<S>3117</S> is Boaz<S>1162</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto her daughter-in<S>1288</S>-law, Blessed be he of Jehovah<S>3068</S>, who hath not left off<S>5800</S> his kindness<S>2617</S> to the living<S>2416</S> and to the dead<S>4191</S>. And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto her<S>376</S>, The man is nigh of kin<S>7138</S> unto us<S>376</S>, one of our near kinsmen<S>1350</S>.

And Ruth<S>7327</S> the Moabitess<S>4125</S> said<S>559</S>, Yea<S>559</S>, he said unto me<S>1692</S>, Thou shalt keep fast by my young men<S>5288</S>, until they have ended<S>3615</S> all my harvest<S>7105</S>.

And Naomi<S>5281</S> said<S>559</S> unto Ruth<S>7327</S> her daughter-in<S>3618</S>-law, It is good<S>2896</S>, my daughter<S>1323</S>, that thou go out<S>3318</S> with his maidens<S>5291</S>, and that they meet<S>6293</S> thee not in any other<S>312</S> field<S>7704</S>.

So she kept fast<S>1692</S> by the maidens<S>5291</S> of Boaz<S>1162</S>, to glean<S>3950</S> unto the end<S>3615</S> of barley<S>8184</S> harvest<S>7105</S> and of wheat<S>2406</S> harvest<S>7105</S>; and she dwelt<S>3427</S> with her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law.
And Naomi<S>5281</S> her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law said<S>559</S> unto her<S>1323</S>, My daughter, shall I not seek<S>1245</S> rest<S>4494</S> for thee<S>3190</S>, that it may be well with thee?

And now is not Boaz<S>1162</S> our kinsman<S>4130</S>, with whose maidens<S>5291</S> thou wast<S>2219</S>? Behold, he winnoweth barley<S>8184</S> to-night<S>3915</S> in the threshing-floor<S>1637</S>.

Wash<S>7364</S> thyself therefore<S>5480</S>, and anoint thee<S>7760</S>, and put thy raiment<S>8071</S> upon thee<S>3381</S>, and get thee down to the threshing-floor<S>1637</S>, but make not thyself known<S>3045</S> unto the man<S>376</S>, until he shall have done<S>3615</S> eating<S>398</S> and drinking<S>8354</S>.

And it shall be<S>7901</S>, when he lieth down, that thou shalt mark<S>3045</S> the place<S>4725</S> where he shall lie<S>7901</S>, and thou shalt go in<S>935</S>, and uncover<S>1540</S> his feet<S>4772</S>, and lay thee down<S>7901</S>; and he will tell<S>5046</S> thee what thou shalt do<S>6213</S>.
And she said<S>559</S> unto her<S>559</S>, All that thou sayest I will do<S>6213</S>.
And she went down<S>3381</S> unto the threshing-floor<S>1637</S>, and did<S>6213</S> according to all that her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law bade her<S>6680</S>.

And when Boaz<S>1162</S> had eaten<S>398</S> and drunk<S>8354</S>, and his heart<S>3820</S> was merry<S>3190</S>, he went<S>935</S> to lie down<S>7901</S> at the end<S>7097</S> of the heap of grain<S>6194</S>: and she came<S>935</S> softly<S>3909</S>, and uncovered<S>1540</S> his feet<S>4772</S>, and laid her down<S>7901</S>.
And it came to pass at midnight<S>2677</S>, that the man<S>376</S> was afraid<S>2729</S>, and turned<S>3943</S> himself<S>802</S>; and, behold, a woman lay<S>7901</S> at his feet<S>4772</S>.

And he said<S>559</S>, Who art thou<S>559</S>? And she answered, I am Ruth<S>7327</S> thy handmaid<S>519</S>: spread<S>6566</S> therefore thy skirt<S>3671</S> over thy handmaid<S>519</S>; for thou art a near kinsman<S>1350</S>.

And he said<S>559</S>, Blessed<S>1288</S> be thou of Jehovah<S>3068</S>, my daughter<S>1323</S>: thou hast showed<S>3190</S> more kindness<S>2617</S> in the latter end<S>314</S> than at the beginning<S>7223</S>, inasmuch as thou followedst<S>3212</S> not<S>1115</S> young men<S>970</S>, whether poor<S>1800</S> or rich<S>6223</S>.

And now<S>1323</S>, my daughter, fear<S>3372</S> not<S>6213</S>; I will do to thee all that thou sayest<S>559</S>; for all the city<S>8179</S> of my people<S>5971</S> doth know<S>3045</S> that thou art a worthy<S>2428</S> woman<S>802</S>.
And now it is true<S>551</S> that I am a near kinsman<S>1350</S>; howbeit there is<S>3426</S> a kinsman<S>1350</S> nearer than I<S>7138</S>.

Tarry<S>3885</S> this night<S>3915</S>, and it shall be in the morning<S>1242</S>, that if he will perform unto thee the part of a kinsman<S>1350</S>, well<S>2896</S>; let him do the kinsman<S>1350</S>'s part: but if he will<S>2654</S> not do the part of a kinsman<S>1350</S> to thee<S>1350</S>, then will I do the part of a kinsman to thee<S>3068</S>, as Jehovah liveth<S>2416</S>: lie down<S>7901</S> until the morning<S>1242</S>.

And she lay<S>7901</S> at his feet<S>4772</S> until the morning<S>1242</S>. And she rose up<S>6965</S> before<S>2958</S> one<S>376</S> could discern<S>5234</S> another<S>7453</S>. For he said<S>559</S>, Let it not be known<S>3045</S> that the woman<S>802</S> came<S>935</S> to the threshing-floor<S>1637</S>.

And he said<S>559</S>, Bring<S>3051</S> the mantle<S>4304</S> that is upon thee<S>270</S>, and hold it<S>270</S>; and she held it<S>4058</S>; and he measured six<S>8337</S> measures of barley<S>8184</S>, and laid<S>7896</S> it on her<S>935</S>: and he went into the city<S>5892</S>.

And when she came<S>935</S> to her mother-in<S>2545</S>-law, she said<S>559</S>, Who art thou<S>1323</S>, my daughter? And she told<S>559</S> her all that the man<S>376</S> had done to her<S>6213</S>.
And she said<S>559</S>, These six<S>8337</S> measures of barley<S>8184</S> gave<S>5414</S> he me<S>559</S>; for he said, Go<S>935</S> not empty<S>7387</S> unto thy mother-in<S>2545</S>-law.

Then said<S>559</S> she<S>3427</S>, Sit still, my daughter<S>1323</S>, until thou know<S>3045</S> how the matter<S>1697</S> will fall<S>5307</S>; for the man<S>376</S> will not rest<S>8252</S>, until<S>518</S> he have finished<S>3615</S> the thing<S>1697</S> this day<S>3117</S>.

Now Boaz<S>1162</S> went<S>5927</S> up<S>5927</S> to the gate<S>8179</S>, and sat him down<S>3427</S> there<S>1350</S>: and, behold, the near kinsman of whom Boaz<S>1162</S> spake<S>1696</S> came<S>5674</S> by<S>559</S>; unto whom he said, Ho<S>1945</S>, such<S>6423</S> a one<S>492</S>! turn aside<S>5493</S>, sit down<S>3427</S> here<S>5493</S>. And he turned aside, and sat down<S>3427</S>.
And he took<S>3947</S> ten<S>6235</S> men<S>582</S> of the elders<S>2205</S> of the city<S>5892</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Sit ye down<S>3427</S> here<S>3427</S>. And they sat down.

And he said<S>559</S> unto the near kinsman<S>1350</S>, Naomi<S>5281</S>, that is come again<S>7725</S> out of the country<S>7704</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>, selleth<S>4376</S> the parcel<S>2513</S> of land<S>7704</S>, which was our brother<S>251</S> Elimelech<S>458</S>'s:

And I thought<S>559</S> to disclose<S>1540</S> it unto thee<S>559</S>, saying, Buy<S>7069</S> it before them<S>3427</S> that sit here<S>2205</S>, and before the elders of my people<S>5971</S>.
